name: Strawberries 'n Cream Cake Roll
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease the bottom and sides of a 15- x 10- x 1-inch jellyroll pan with vegetable oil. Line with wax paper and grease the wax paper with oil. Set aside.
2. Beat egg yolks in a large bowl at high speed using an electric mixer until thick and lemon-colored. Stir in vanilla extract. Set aside.
3. Beat egg whites until foamy. Add cream of tartar and salt, beating until stiff but not dry. Gradually fold in 3/4 cup powdered sugar. Fold the mixture into the yolks. Gradually fold in flour. Spread batter evenly into prepared pan.
4. Bake at 350°F for 10 to 12 minutes. Sift 1 to 2 tablespoons powdered sugar over a cloth towel on a cutting board.
5. When done, loosen the cake from the sides of the pan and turn it out onto the sugared towel. Peel off wax paper. Starting at the narrow end, roll up the cake and towel together. Let cool completely on a wire rack, seam side down.
6. Combine sliced strawberries with 2 tablespoons sugar. Beat whipping cream until foamy. Gradually add 3 tablespoons sugar, beating until soft peaks form.
7. Unroll the cake. Spread the cake with sliced strawberries and half of the whipped cream. Reroll the cake without the towel. Place on a serving plate, seam side down. Spread remaining whipped cream on all sides. Garnish with whole strawberries if desired. Chill before serving. Yield: 8 to 10 servings.
